user_query:
I’m coordinating a cross‑time‑zone production check‑in with my Paris co‑producer and need a short list of concrete options that everyone can make. Please do the following using the attendees listed in input/attendees.csv.

Goal and constraints:
- Propose exactly three 60‑minute meeting slots during Monday–Friday of 2025‑05‑12 through 2025‑05‑16 that all attendees can make. Each slot must:
  • fall entirely within every attendee’s local working window for that date (from the CSV),
  • avoid weekends,
  • avoid public holidays in both Mexico (country code MX) and France (FR) for 2025, and
  • be selected deterministically as the three earliest feasible slots in ascending UTC order across the week.
- Do not hardcode time zone offsets or holidays. Use no‑auth public sources and save what you used.

Internet requirements (do not include direct URLs):
- Fetch 2025 public holidays for FR and MX from the Nager.Date public holidays API (official project/domain pattern: *.nager.at) and save each full JSON response to:
  • web/holidays_FR_2025.json
  • web/holidays_MX_2025.json
- Obtain UTC offset information for the IANA zones in the CSV (Europe/Paris and America/Mexico_City) for each date in 2025‑05‑12 to 2025‑05‑16 using a no‑auth time zone API that returns JSON (e.g., domain patterns timeapi.io or worldtimeapi.org). For each zone, compile a per‑date map of the UTC offset you will use (evaluate the offset at 12:00 local time for that zone on each date) and save to:
  • web/time_offsets_Europe_Paris_2025-05-12_2025-05-16.json
  • web/time_offsets_America_Mexico_City_2025-05-12_2025-05-16.json

Deliverables (write structured results to local files):
- outputs/proposals.csv: a table of the three selected slots with columns:
  slot_id, date (YYYY-MM-DD), utc_start, utc_end, america_mexico_city_start, america_mexico_city_end, europe_paris_start, europe_paris_end, is_holiday_FR (true/false), is_holiday_MX (true/false), all_within_working_hours (true/false).
  Use ISO 8601 timestamps with a Z suffix for UTC columns and explicit numeric offsets for local time columns.
- outputs/summary.json: a summary that includes:
  • considered_dates (array of YYYY‑MM‑DD),
  • excluded_dates with reasons per date (e.g., weekend, FR holiday, MX holiday, no overlapping window),
  • tz_offsets_used mapping each date to the UTC offsets you applied for Europe/Paris and America/Mexico_City,
  • sources listing the exact file paths of the downloaded holiday JSON files and the compiled per‑zone offset JSON files, and
  • selected_slots echoing the three proposals (slot_id and the same timestamps as in the CSV).

Validation/cross‑checks:
- selected_slots in outputs/summary.json must correspond exactly to the three rows in outputs/proposals.csv by slot_id and timestamps.
- Do not propose any slot on a date that appears as a holiday in either web/holidays_FR_2025.json or web/holidays_MX_2025.json.
- Do not propose any slot that would place any attendee outside their local working_start/working_end from the CSV.

Input path:
- input/attendees.csv (provided).

Please keep all new files under web/ and outputs/ as specified, and ensure the workflow is reproducible from the saved inputs and downloaded JSON. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
